rationale: Realistic benchmark for AI agents to parse Markdown files, extract meaningful metadata without processing code blocks or archival quotes, and produce a JSON summary relevant to release engineering.
task_description: Read the provided Markdown file, ignore any fenced code blocks or quoted archival notes, and generate a JSON summary containing the document title, the first-level headers, and the total count of second-level headers.
input_markdown:
# Release Notes for Version 2.1.0

## Overview

This release includes several important enhancements and bug fixes.

## New Features

### User Interface Improvements

The dashboard has been redesigned for better usability.

### Performance Optimizations

Backend response times have been reduced by 30%.

## Bug Fixes

### Resolved Issues

Fixed login timeout error occurring under high load.

### Known Issues

Intermittent profile save failure still under investigation.

```python
# Archived: legacy middleware module (to be deprecated)
import sys

def legacy_handler():
    print("Deprecated handler called")
```

## Documentation

### Updated Guides

All user manuals have been updated to reflect recent changes.

### Archived Notes

> The following notes are preserved for historical reference only.
> Module `auth` API v1.3 deprecated as of 2023-01-01.

## Support

### Contact Information

For enterprise support email support@company.example.com

### License

See LICENSE.md for complete licensing details.
scorer_policy: Exact match of JSON structure and values. Overall structure must be an object containing exactly the keys: document_title (string), first_level_headers (array of strings), and second_level_header_count (integer). Nested objects or arrays are not permitted. All string values must match exactly, including whitespace.
